items.title: National Trust SA faces inquiry motion amid $230,000 property dispute

children.content: Another National of Trust South Australia branch goes public about its financial dispute with the high-profile heritage group, as the state opposition announces it will refer members' concerns to a parliamentary committee.

gallery.altText: A middle aged man in a T shirt standing in front of several historical cars

thumbnail.altText: A middle aged man in a T shirt standing in front of several historical cars

items.title: SA premier plays down social media posts attempting to reveal his home address

children.content: SA Premier Peter Malinauskas has played down social media posts attempting to reveal his home address in the aftermath of hundreds of trees being removed for the $45 million North Adelaide Golf Course.

image.altText: Premier Peter Malinauskas is wearing a suit and standing in front of new homes

items.title: Overseas company makes billions in profit but is receiving millions in government bailouts

children.content: China is "playing a hard game" with its stranglehold on the global minerals and metals market and Australian smelters need taxpayer support to "play hard back".

image.altText: Two large white buildings at a metal smelter by a port.

items.title: 'Predator' arsonist's $1.5m fire an 'act of retribution', court told

children.content: The guilty man embarked on a two-week crime spree after his relationship soured with his business partner, who he also assaulted in front of the man's children.

avatar.alt: A woman with blonde hair wearing a black blazer and white top

items.title: PM 'confident' about Whyalla amid work to bring furnace 'back to life'

children.content: Anthony Albanese says he remains "very confident" in the future of steelmaking in Whyalla, and says the federal government is "continuing to work" with lead producer Nyrstar to secure its operations in Port Pirie and Hobart.

image.altText: A view of the Whyalla steelworks.

items.title: Steel and lead are facing trial by fire in SA's regional cities

children.content: The South Australian government has put all its chips on the table for the Whyalla steelworks and Port Pirie lead smelter. The next six months will be critical.

image.altText: A worker at the face of the blast furnace at the Whyalla steelworks.

items.title: 'This is the end': SA health chief recalls terrifying moment she was hit by car

children.content: SA public health chief Nicola Spurrier opens up about the moment when she was knocked from her bike by a car last year, and recounted the experience — which left her with broken bones and fearing for her life — only hours after a 39-year-old motorcyclist was killed in a crash in the Adelaide Hills.

image.altText: A woman pulls the top of her blouse aside, showing a 5-centimetre scar on her left shoulder

items.title: Students of newly merged Adelaide University protest changing of graduation venue

children.content: The newly merged Adelaide University has changed the venue of its graduation ceremonies from Bonython Hall, to Pridham Hall with students launching a petition in opposition.

image.altText: Bonython Hall - an old heritage brick building in the Adelaide CBD

items.title: Elk have been spotted in the wild in SA. Where did they come from?

children.content: A South Australian landowner has spotted elk on her property, outside containment lines of deer farms. There are no known established wild elk herds in this state.

gallery.altText: two large elk stand next to bushland.

thumbnail.altText: two large elk stand next to bushland.

items.title: Stronger mouse poison allowed for 'worst plague on record'

children.content: Farm groups have welcomed the chemical regulator's long-awaited decision to approve double-strength mouse baits amid plague numbers in Western Australia and South Australia.

image.altText: Three mice in farm shed.

items.title: First Nations art collective calls for industry inquiry to protect artists

children.content: APY Art Centre Collective members say a voluntary code of conduct isn't protecting artists from financial exploitation and misconduct in the industry.

image.altText: The exterior of a building with corrugated metal sheets and a bright red logo, reading APY Art Centre Collective

tags.title: Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Art

items.title: 'I wish I were dead': The heartbreaking toll of Australia's favourite pastime

children.content: Australians are the world's biggest gamblers, losing billions every year. But the true cost goes far beyond the financial.

gallery.altText: A photo of a middle-aged man leaning back on a sofa with his head in his hands.

thumbnail.altText: A photo of a middle-aged man leaning back on a sofa with his head in his hands.

items.title: Concerns raised tobacco giant allowed to push for excise cut in private

children.content: A public health academic has raised concerns a tobacco giant was allowed to lobby for a cut to the tobacco excise in private. Follow live.

image.altText: A male hand holding a smoking cigarette, in front of dark background.

items.title: Stranded students rescued from flooding in national park

children.content: Almost 50 high school students and teachers have been rescued after becoming stranded by floodwater at a national park in the Scenic Rim.

image.altText: A mountain range in regional Queensland.

items.title: The parts of Victoria adding the biggest share to disaster damage bills

children.content: At least $57 billion worth of infrastructure in Victoria is vulnerable to more frequent and severe bushfires, floods and extreme heat. This figure is expected to grow.

items.title: Elon Musk loses lawsuit against OpenAI and Sam Altman

children.content: The Tesla boss accused OpenAI of wrongfully trying to enrich investors and insiders at the nonprofit's expense, and failing to prioritise AI's safety.

image.altText: A composite image showing Elon Musk and Sam Altman's faces.

items.title: 'Two years to live': Why we used our super for weight loss surgery

children.content: Weight loss surgery has become an increasingly common option for people grappling with obesity — and many are funding it using retirement savings.

image.altText: A man with glasses, a white t-shirt and a sleeve tattoo, stands beside a woman with glasses and a dark blue top.

items.title: What Kylie Minogue learned from Michael Hutchence and Nick Cave

children.content: Australia's reigning Queen of Pop discusses her relationships with Nick Cave and the late Michael Hutchence, and being tapped to headline the AFL grand final's pre-game entertainment.

items.title: Israeli forces intercept Gaza flotilla with Australians on board, organisers say

children.content: The organisers of a flotilla of ships trying to break Israel's maritime blockade of Gaza say Israel's navy is intercepting the group off the coast of Cyprus.

items.title: Bodies of four missing Italian scuba divers found in underwater cave in Maldives

children.content: The bodies of four missing Italian scuba divers who died last week have been found in an underwater cave in the Maldives.

image.altText: Side-by-side images of a mother (left) and her daughter.

items.title: When the law failed them, these men confronted the thief who stole their movie

children.content: What do you do when your stolen film wins a slew of awards for the thief? In the case of these filmmakers, you travel across the globe to hunt him down.

items.title: Today's quiz tests you on iconic movie quotes. You. Shall. (Probably.) Pass!

children.content: The ABC Timelines quiz is back to test your knowledge of some of the most iconic movie quotes of the 2000s.

items.title: What you can do today to ease money stress in your relationship

children.content: While financial tension between partners isn't always about money, many people clash over values, spending habits, and how they respond to financial pressure.

image.altText: family in kitchen with baby and child

items.title: A secret mouse experiment in the 1990s threatened Brisbane's water supply

children.content: Brisbane was days away from running out of drinkable water in the 1990s due to a secret mouse experiment that was kept hidden from the public … until now.

items.title: Granny flat ban lifted for some residents under airport flight path

children.content: The NSW government will permit secondary dwellings such as granny flats in some areas affected by noise from Western Sydney International Airport.

image.altText: A dual occupancy home with a white mailbox that has two openings that read "A" and "B".

items.title: Woman dies after being hit by ute on Pacific Motorway

children.content: Police say initial enquiries indicate a female was out of her vehicle when she was struck.

image.altText: Close up image of the lapel of a Queensland police officer

items.title: Shops gutted after suspicious fire at Melbourne butcher and grocery

children.content: Fire crews were called to a blaze in Glenroy that gutted several shops. Locals say there have been multiple suspicious fires in the area.

image.altText: Firefighters with a hose and a chainsaw.

items.title: PM knocks back $94 million proposal to help flooded farmers restock

children.content: Widespread flooding in north-west Queensland killed more than 90,000 head of livestock this year, but farmers will have to go it alone if they want to rebuild.

image.altText: Cattle eating hay on muddy ground surrounded by floodwaters

items.title: There's no cure and no treatment for MND, but finally there is hope

children.content: Natalie has seen her body and speech deteriorate; she has lost her independence and dignity — but she says new research into motor neurone disease has given her hope for those diagnosed in the future.

items.title: Two men arrested over stunt at Punch the monkey's enclosure

children.content: One of the men was detained by a zookeeper and arrested after allegedly climbing a fence and breaking into the enclosure.

image.altText: A baby Japanese macaque next to an Ikea stuffed orangutan toy.

items.title: Spanish PM's Socialists party on notice after historic defeat in stronghold

children.content: Voters in the region of Andalusia have sent the ruling Spain's Socialists yet another warning, adding to concerns Pedro Sánchez's government could suffer an electoral wipe-out in a nationwide vote.

image.altText: Pedro Sanchez on a campaign stage clapping and smiling.

items.title: The dead fish waste disaster that Tasmania failed to plan for

children.content: High-volume salmon mortalities last year swamped Tasmania's waste streams as companies scrambled to find somewhere to accept them, documents show. There are now concerns the state remains unprepared for future mass deaths.

gallery.altText: a skip bin with salmon carcasses

thumbnail.altText: a skip bin with salmon carcasses

items.title: Journalist in London stabbed at behest of Iran, court told

children.content: Pouria Zaratifoukolaei, who works for a Persian-language outlet critical of the Iranian government, was attacked near his home in London in 2024.

image.altText: A court sketch of two men with dark hair wearing smart attire behind persplex glass.

items.title: It's time for Eric Abetz to tackle Tasmania's debt. How far will he go?

children.content: The lead-up to state budget day is normally a time for flashy funding announcements. But as Tasmania stares down the barrel of $7 billion of debt, things are looking a bit bleaker.

image.altText: A politician speaking to journalists in a room.
